text: PSA: if you're seeing intermittent 503s from the auth service, we know about it. The connection pool is exhausting under load. Workaround: restart your local instance. Permanent fix is in the backlog but no ETA. This has been happening for 3 weeks now.

Just got off a call with our second-largest account. They're threatening to churn because response times on support tickets have gone from 4 hours to 2 days. We don't have enough support engineers and the knowledge base is so outdated that new support hires can't self-serve answers.

Compiled Q1 customer feedback from Gong calls. Top 3 themes: (1) API docs are wrong/missing - mentioned in 40% of calls, (2) pricing model doesn't fit non-SaaS use cases, (3) implementation takes too long vs competitors. I've been sharing these monthly but engineering says they never see them. Where is the disconnect?

INCIDENT: Production database failover triggered. Primary DB CPU at 100% for 15 minutes. Automatic failover to replica succeeded but 2 minutes of write errors occurred. Root cause: unoptimized query from the new reporting feature deployed yesterday. No load testing was done before deploy.

Hey team, I've been trying to get the integration test suite running locally for 3 days now. The README says to use Docker Compose but the compose file references images that don't exist anymore. I asked in #help but got conflicting answers. Is there an up-to-date guide somewhere? Starting to feel like I'm wasting everyone's time asking basic questions.

Lost another deal today. Prospect asked for a SOC2 report and we couldn't produce one. Also asked about our SLA guarantees and I had to say 'we don't have formal SLAs documented.' These are table-stakes requirements for enterprise deals. How many deals do we need to lose before we invest in compliance?
